concerns.text: FR-003 mandates training a 'Cycle-Agnostic fallback model (GSN-only)' for unseen cycles. While T020 mentions applying a fallback, there is no explicit task to IMPLEMENT or TRAIN this specific fallback model architecture. The plan rejects categorical Cycle IDs, but the spec requires a distinct GSN-only model for pre-satellite cycles. This implementation gap leaves FR-003 unfulfilled.

FR-002 requires 'linear interpolation for gaps < 1 year'. Task T014 describes 'Linear interpolation for gaps < 1 year' but conflates the logic with the fixed baseline for larger gaps in a single bullet, lacking a distinct task or clear separation for the short-gap interpolation logic required by the spec's dual-threshold approach.

FR-009 and SC-005 require a sensitivity analysis sweeping the 'inconsistency tolerance threshold' (defined as max diff between cycle-specific calibration coefficients). The plan and tasks (T029) implement a sweep, but since the plan explicitly rejects 'cycle-specific calibration coefficients' (using Cycle Phase sin/cos instead), there is no task to define or calculate the specific metric required by FR-009 to perform the sweep. The task exists, but the underlying data element it sweeps is missing from the design.

US-1 Independent Test requires 'leave-one-cycle-out' validation. Task T015 implements 'Time-Block Cross-Validation (Train: 2003-2015, Test: 2016-Present)'. There is no task explicitly implementing the 'leave-one-cycle-out' strategy mandated by the spec's US-1 Independent Test and FR-003 context, creating a coverage gap for the specified validation method.

Task T014 description conflates the gap-filling logic for <1 year and >=1 year gaps into a single bullet point, making it ambiguous whether the linear interpolation step is fully covered as a distinct implementation requirement per FR-002.
